sourceTitle: A connected workspace for product teams to collaborate, ideate and launch

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of turning a Notion page into a wiki for a product team?

options.text: It automatically syncs the page with external GitHub repositories.
options.explanation: Syncing with GitHub is a separate embed feature, not a wiki function.

options.text: It adds verification properties that help keep content up‑to‑date.
options.explanation: Turning a page into a wiki adds a verification property, allowing owners to set expiration periods and receive notifications.

options.text: It converts the page into a Kanban board view.
options.explanation: Board views are a database view option, unrelated to the wiki toggle.

options.text: It locks the page so only admins can edit it.
options.explanation: Wiki mode does not lock editing; it only adds verification and layout options.

questions.question: Which Notion feature allows you to embed live content from tools like GitHub, Jira, or Figma into a project page?

options.text: Linked databases
options.explanation: Linked databases surface Notion data elsewhere; they don’t embed external tool content.

options.text: Page verification
options.explanation: Verification tracks content freshness, not embedding.

options.text: Embeds
options.explanation: The embed block lets you bring files, videos, and live views from external tools directly onto a Notion page.

options.text: Buttons
options.explanation: Buttons automate actions inside Notion but don’t embed external content.

questions.question: How can a product team ensure that a document template (e.g., PRD) stays consistent across the workspace?

options.text: Create a database template for that document type.
options.explanation: Database templates standardize structure and fields, making every new document follow the same format.

options.text: Manually copy‑paste the same layout each time.
options.explanation: Manual copying is error‑prone and defeats the purpose of templates.

options.text: Use the @mention feature to tag the document.
options.explanation: @mentions are for linking people or pages, not for standardizing layouts.

options.text: Enable page verification for the template page.
options.explanation: Verification checks freshness, not consistency of structure.

questions.question: What advantage does a relation property provide between a Tasks database and a Projects database?

options.text: It creates a two‑way link so each task knows its parent project and each project lists its tasks.
options.explanation: Relation properties establish bidirectional connections, giving visibility from both sides.

options.text: It automatically assigns tasks to team members based on project priority.
options.explanation: Assignment requires a person property; relations only link records.

options.text: It converts the task list into a timeline view.
options.explanation: Views are separate settings; relations don’t change the view type.

options.text: It merges the two databases into a single table.
options.explanation: Relations keep databases separate while linking records, not merging them.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a way to display the same Notion database in different contexts?

options.text: Creating a linked database on another page.
options.explanation: Linked databases let you surface the same data elsewhere.

options.text: Changing the database view to board, table, or timeline.
options.explanation: Switching views is a built‑in way to display data differently.

options.text: Exporting the database to a CSV file and re‑importing it.
options.explanation: Exporting creates a static copy; it does not display the live database in multiple places.

options.text: Syncing a block that contains part of the database.
options.explanation: Block sync replicates content across pages while staying linked.

questions.question: How does the 'Report a Bug' button improve the workflow for engineers?

options.text: It sends an email notification to the engineering lead.
options.explanation: Buttons can trigger actions, but the guide describes creating a database entry, not sending emails.

options.text: It creates a new database entry for the bug directly from the product homepage.
options.explanation: The button automates the creation of a bug record, streamlining reporting.

options.text: It automatically assigns the bug to the next sprint.
options.explanation: Assignment requires additional configuration; the button only creates the entry.

options.text: It opens a third‑party bug‑tracking tool in a new tab.
options.explanation: The button works within Notion to generate a record, not to launch external tools.

questions.question: What is the purpose of using the 'Turn to Wiki' option on a top‑level page?

options.text: To enable page verification and additional layout options such as table or list views.
options.explanation: Turning a page into a wiki adds verification and lets you view pages in database‑style layouts.

options.text: To lock the page so only admins can edit it.
options.explanation: Wiki mode does not restrict editing permissions.

options.text: To automatically sync the page with a Google Docs file.
options.explanation: Syncing with Google Docs is not a built‑in wiki feature.

options.text: To convert the page into a Kanban board.
options.explanation: Kanban board views are set on databases, not on a simple wiki toggle.

questions.question: When setting up recurring meeting notes, which Notion feature allows the template to auto‑create entries at regular intervals?

options.text: Relation property
options.explanation: Relations link records; they don’t schedule recurrence.

options.text: Repeating template (template with a repeat schedule)
options.explanation: A repeating template can be configured to generate new entries weekly, bi‑weekly, etc.

options.text: Button that copies the page
options.explanation: Buttons can create new pages but don’t inherently handle timed recurrence.

options.text: Page verification
options.explanation: Verification tracks freshness, not creation frequency.

questions.question: Why might a product team choose to embed a GitHub repository directly into a Notion project page rather than linking to it?

options.text: Embedding shows the latest version of files without leaving Notion, keeping context in one place.
options.explanation: Embedding provides live, in‑context view of external content, reducing context‑switching.

options.text: Embedding automatically grants all Notion users write access to the repository.
options.explanation: Permissions are controlled in GitHub, not by the embed.

options.text: Embedding compresses the repository size to improve Notion performance.
options.explanation: Embedding displays content; it does not alter repository size.

options.text: Embedding converts the code into a PDF for easier reading.
options.explanation: Embedding shows the live view or file preview, not a PDF conversion.

questions.question: Which combination of Notion features best supports turning meeting notes into actionable tasks?

options.text: Page verification + linked database
options.explanation: Verification tracks freshness; it doesn’t create tasks.

options.text: Relation property + button that creates a task entry
options.explanation: A relation links notes to projects, and a button can instantly add a new task to the tasks database.

options.text: Template button + timeline view
options.explanation: Template buttons help create pages; timeline view is for visualizing dates, not task creation.

options.text: Multi‑select tags + page verification
options.explanation: Tags help categorize, verification checks recency; neither creates tasks directly.
